title: 1138 (easter egg)
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Youmay|the easter egg|[[1138 (disambiguation)|other uses]]}}
[[File:THX1138-Poster.jpg|thumb|right|200px]]
'''1138''' is a number that appears in products that are related to [[George Lucas]] and his works. This recurrent in-joke in ''Star Wars'' films and [[LucasArts]] computer games originates in Lucas' short student film [[Wikipedia:Electronic Labyrinth: THX 1138 4EB|''Electronic Labyrinth: THX 1138 4EB'']], which was later reworked into his first theatrical release, [[Wikipedia:THX 1138|''THX 1138'']]. The number appears in several ''Star Wars'' productions in honor of the director's previous works.<ref name="SW">{{SW|url=news/beyond-a-cell-block-references-to-thx-1138-in-star-wars|text=Beyond a Cell Block: References to ''THX 1138'' in ''Star Wars''|date=September 15, 2015|nolive=1}}</ref>

==References==
[[1138 (disambiguation)|Many elements]] in ''Star Wars'' include 1138 in their names, and it is a frequent [[Easter egg (virtual)|easter egg]] in ''Star Wars'' products:
===Films===
====Original trilogy====
*In the film {{Film|4}}, when [[Shann Childsen|an Imperial officer]] questions [[Luke Skywalker]] and [[Han Solo]] about [[Chewbacca]], Luke attempts to fool him by claiming it is a "[[Prisoner]] transfer from [[Cell Block 1138|Cell Block '''1138''']]."<ref name="SW" />
**Shortly afterward, Han says to Luke, "We gotta find out which cell this [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|princess]] of yours is in. Here it is. '''[[Cell 2187|2187]]'''."<ref name="ANH">{{Film|4}}</ref>
**When [[C-3PO]] asks what he and [[R2-D2]] should do if they are discovered, the first line on the monitor behind them reads "'''THX/1138'''".<ref name="SW" />
*In the film {{Film|5}}, shortly before the [[Battle of Hoth]], [[General]] [[Carlist Rieekan|Rieekan]] orders [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogues 10]] and [[Rogue Eleven/Legends|'''11''']] to Station '''3-8'''.<ref name="SW" />
*In {{Film|6}}, '''1138''' is inscribed on the side of [[Boushh]]'s [[helmet]].<ref name="SW" /><ref name="Force">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/collecting/story/ForceCast_Boushh_Mystery_Solved_110334.asp|text=Force-Cast: Boushh Mystery Solved|date=2007-11-22|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20221022065930}}</ref>

====Prequel trilogy====
[[File:1138Binks-TPM.png|thumb|right|250px|1138 in ''The Phantom Menace'']]
*In {{Film|1}}, the identification number '''[[1138 (Trade Federation)|1138]]''' is printed on the back of the [[comlink booster pack]] of the [[B1-series battle droid|B1 battle droid]] that [[Jar Jar Binks|Jar Jar]] pushes over the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship|droid control ship]] is shut down.<ref name="SW" /> The number is written in [[Trade Federation Script]], but can easily be deciphered even without a guide.<ref name="TPM">{{Film|1}}</ref>
*In {{Film|2}}, '''[[1138 (Geonosis)|1138]]''' is printed on the back of the booster back of a B1 battle droid that is firing at opposing [[Jedi]] while [[Mace Windu]] deflects a blaster shot into a [[OOM command battle droid]] during the [[First Battle of Geonosis|Battle of Geonosis]]. This number is also written in Trade Federation Script, but is easily deciphered without a guide.<ref name="Episode II">{{Film|2}}</ref> The numbers are officially identified in ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition]]''.<ref>''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>
**Though not legible in the film itself,<ref name="Episode II" /> the [[clone trooper]]s in the film have red LED lights in the back of their [[Clone trooper armor|helmets]] that read '''1138'''.<ref name="SW" />
*Though never stated in the film,<ref name="SW" /> the script for {{Film|3}} identifies [[Clone Marshal Commander]] Bacara, one of the clones that [[Order 66|shoots down]] [[Ki-Adi-Mundi]] on [[Mygeeto]], as [[Bacara|CC-'''1138''']].<ref name="SWArchive2">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/release/video/f20040909/index.html?page=2|text=Looking for THX 1138 in a Galaxy Far, Far Away…|date=2004-09-09}} {{C|Page 2 of 4}}</ref> This made its way into [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] reference books such as ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE 1138">{{CSWECite|I|54|Bacara, Commander}}</ref> Bacara's designation was confirmed as [[canon]] in the [[Databank (2014-)|Databank]] on [[StarWars.com]].<ref name="Clone Commander Bacara">{{Databank|clone-commander-bacara|Clone Commander Bacara}}</ref>

====Sequel trilogy====
*During the [[Attack on Tuanul|attack on the Jakku village]] in {{Film|7}}, there is a [[First Order stormtrooper]] called [[FN-3181]] (not referred to by name in the film itself but is identified in the credits), which is 1138 rearranged.<ref name="TFA">{{Film|7}}</ref>
**According to the [[StarWars.com]] article "From "Blast That X-wing!" to "Traitor!": The Voices of ''Star Wars: The Force Awakens''", during the same attack, another stormtrooper is heard saying "Check over there.", with another replying "Fan out!", and the first one answering "Copy that, [[1138 (First Order)|'''1138''']]".<ref name="Voices">{{SW|url=news/from-blast-that-x-wing-to-traitor-the-voices-of-star-wars-the-force-awakens|text=From "Blast that X-Wing!" to "Traitor!": The voices of ''Star Wars: The Force Awakens''|date=2016-01-25}}</ref> However, this cannot be heard in the final product.<ref name="TFA" />

===Live-action series===
====''Andor''====
*'''1138''' is printed on the side of the [[HFY-3920 shuttle ferry|shuttle ferry]] in the ''[[Star Wars: Andor]]'' episode "[[Reckoning (episode)|Reckoning]]."<ref name="Reck">{{Andor|Reckoning (episode)}}</ref>

====''Star Wars: Skeleton Crew''====
*In the ''[[Star Wars: Skeleton Crew]]'' episode "[[We're Gonna Be In So Much Trouble]]," while inside one of the vaults in the [[At Attin Mint]], a [[safety droid]] notes that there are "one thousand, one hundred and thirty nine" vaults in total,<ref name="So Much Trouble">{{SkeletonCrew|We're Gonna Be In So Much Trouble}}</ref> meaning that there are '''1138''' more; a reference to ''THX-1138''.<ref name="So Much Trouble Trivia">{{SW|url=season-1-episode-7-trivia-gallery-skeleton-crew|text="We're Gonna Be In So Much Trouble" Trivia Gallery | Star Wars: Skeleton Crew|quote=The security droid notes there are 1,139 vaults, meaning there are 1,138 more. The number is a nod to George Lucas' THX-1138, and often used in Star Wars lore.}} {{C|slide 1}}</ref>

===Animated series===
====''Star Wars: The Clone Wars''====
*In the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode "[[Innocents of Ryloth]]," when [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] operates the [[proton cannon]], the [[Aurebesh]] in the upper left corner of the screen reads "'''X''': '''11''' Y: '''38'''".<ref>{{TCW|Innocents of Ryloth}}</ref>
*In "[[Liberty on Ryloth]]," a [[Galactic Republic|Republic]] display includes the [[Aurebesh]] text "'''X 11''' Y '''38'''.<ref>{{TCW|Liberty on Ryloth}}</ref>
*In "[[The Zillo Beast Strikes Back]]," the side of the platform the [[Zillo Beast|beast]] is on at the beginning of the episode reads "1381", which is 1138 rearranged.<ref>{{TCW|The Zillo Beast Strikes Back}}</ref>
*In "[[Clone Cadets]]," [[Clone Commander]] [[Colt]] gives orders to test the [[Domino Squad]] in the [[Citadel Challenge]] in version '''THX''' variable '''1138'''.<ref name="SW" /><ref>{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/clone-cadets-trivia-gallery|text=Clone Cadets Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 3}}</ref>
*In "[[The Citadel (episode)|The Citadel]]," the [[Shuttle 81572|cargo shuttle]] used by the Jedi is named "'''1138'''".<ref>{{TCW|The Citadel (episode)}}</ref>

====''Star Wars: The Bad Batch''====
*In the episode "[[The Summit]]," a sensor technician identifies the sensor that [[Tech]] had temporarily disables as sensor '''38-11'''.

====''Star Wars Rebels''====
*In the ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' episode "[[Rebel Resolve]]," the [[Imperial officer]]'s monitor contains a prisoner list written in Aurebesh. The first prisoner listed is "[[Lord|LADY]] [[M'Arshngton|M'ARSHNGTON]]" • "TK-'''1138'''".<ref>{{Rebels|Rebel Resolve}}</ref>

===''LEGO Star Wars''===
*In the non-canon animated special ''[[LEGO Star Wars Terrifying Tales]]'', [[Graballa]] the [[Hutt]] is pursued into the [[toilet]] by an axe-wielding [[B1-series battle droid|battle droid]] named [[B-14961138815|B-1496'''1138'''815]].<ref>''[[LEGO Star Wars Terrifying Tales]]''</ref>
*In the non-canon animated special ''[[LEGO Star Wars Summer Vacation]]'', the sunscreen used by [[Darth Sidious]] says "[[SPF 1138|SPF '''1138''']]" in [[Aurebesh]].
*The C-3PO [[LEGO]] set 75398 has 1138 pieces.

===Games===
====Legends games====
*In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] the player has to inspect in Mission One of Battle Three a set of suspicious-looking containers labeled '''THX-113'''6A, '''THX-113'''7D, '''THX-1138'''B, '''THX-113'''9D and '''THX-11'''40D.<ref>[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref><ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--July 1994-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]'', there is a [[CR90 corvette]] called '''''[[1138 (CR90 corvette)|1138]]'''''.<ref>''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]''</ref> <!--April 1997-->
*In [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], during the Battle of [[Dac/Legends|Calamari]] mission, a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] says "Fighters inbound on '''one'''-decimal-'''one-three-eight'''."<ref>[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref> <!--December 1998-->	
*In ''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]],'' the basic infantry unit of the Empire - the Imperial Stormtrooper - upon selection will state "'''THX-1138, ready sir."<!--20 March, 2000-->'''	
*In ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', the Imperial stormtrooper unit answers "'''THX-1138''' ready, sir!" when selected.<ref>''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> <!--November 2001-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'', '''[[1-138]]''' is the designation of an automated hauler that [[Kyle Katarn]] flies to the [[Cairn Installation]] in the [[Lenico Belt]]. On the 19th mission, the code needed to contact [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] is '''T-H-X''' in Aurebesh.<ref>''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''</ref> <!--March 26, 2002-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'', [[Spacestation 1138|Spacestation '''1138''']] is a [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] prison.<ref>''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''</ref> <!--March 10, 2002-->
*In [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']], there is a [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] known as '''[[Jedi training droid/Legends#Training Droid 1138|1138]]'''.<ref>[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']]</ref> <!--December 2001-->
*In [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], the access code for one of the laboratories in the [[Geonosian Bio-Lab]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] is '''1138'''0. A stormtrooper located in the [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] [[Quarantine Zone (Dathomir)|Quarantine Zone]] had the identification number '''[[HX-138]]'''.<ref>[[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']]</ref> <!--June 2003-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', in single player campaign mode, on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the player pilots a stolen [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]. During the mission, the main path divides in two. If the player proceeds down the path on the right, they'll have to destroy a few enemy AT-STs along the way. One of them will shout "Unit '''1138''' what are you doing?!!" just as the player opens fire on them.<ref>''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref> <!--October 2003-->
*In the video game ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'', [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Boss (clone commando)/Legends|Boss]] is designated RC-'''1138'''. During the ''[[Prosecutor]]'' stage of the game, [[Commando/Legends|Republic Commando]] [[Scorch/Legends|Scorch]] is taken to the [[Assault ship/Legends|assault ship's]] detention block and is held in [[Cell 2187 (Prosecutor)|cell '''2187''']].<ref name="SW" /> <!--February 2005-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'', [[Galen Marek]] is referred to as "Subject '''1138'''" during the escape from the ''[[Empirical]]''. In [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] the ship that [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]] stands by at the end has '''THX 1138''' in it. In the Databank, the [[Load Spider THK 421|"Load Spider" '''THX''' 421 Cargo Shuttle]] is said to have been based on the design of the [[THX 1138 model|'''THX 1138''' model]].<ref>''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref><!--September 2008-->
*In ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'', in campaign mode, on Endor, the player to destroy an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]]. The pilot's name is '''[[List of computer-controlled character names in Battlefront games|TH 113]]'''.<ref>''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]''</ref> <!--November 2009-->
*In the sequel ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'', Talus Haroon 10-'''11-38''' is the clearance code to dock on the [[Salvation (flagship)|''Salvation'']].<ref name="SW" /> As '''X''' is the Roman numeral for '''10''', you basically have '''T'''alus '''H'''aroon '''X-11-38''' <!--October 2010-->
*In ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', [[Section 1138|Section '''1138''']] is a law that is a part of the [[Galactic Republic Charter]]. Many panel displays have the same basic block of generic tech chatter streaming across them in Aurebesh font. One frequently found block of such streaming text translates to "ALL SYSTEMS ACTIVE, RECHARGE [[Power cell/Legends|POWER-CELL]] '''1138''' AT FULL POWER PROCEED AFTER SHUT-DOWN, WILL PROCEED AS SCHEDULED."<ref>''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> <!--December 2011-->
*In ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]'', the main reactor of the ''[[Nihilist]]'' is on Level '''1138'''.<ref>''[[Kinect Star Wars]]''</ref> <!--April 2012-->

====Canon games====
*In the tutorial for the browser game ''[[Star Wars Rebels: Ghost Raid]]'', the score in the top left corner is 00'''1138'''.<ref>''[[Star Wars Rebels: Ghost Raid]]''</ref> <!--July 2014-->
*In the world-building game ''[[Disney Magic Kingdoms]]'', there is a [[First Order]] stormtrooper called [[KT-1138|KT-'''1138''']].<ref>''[[Disney Magic Kingdoms]]''</ref> <!--March 2016-->

===Novels===
====Legends novels====
*In the [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|novelization]] of ''A New Hope'', '''THX-1138''' is the designation of the stormtrooper referred to as [[TK-421]] in the movie.<ref name="SW" /> <!--November 1976-->
*In ''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]'', an illustration shows a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] stenciled with the designation "'''THX-1'''" on its side.<ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--June 1992-->
*In ''[[Queen of the Empire]]'', upon their arrival at [[Hologram Fun World]], Han, Leia and the droids are greeted by thrilling music that emanates from '''1''','''138''' [[THX Ultrasound Speaker|'''THX''' Ultrasound Speakers]].<ref>''[[Queen of the Empire]]''</ref> <!--March 1993-->
*In ''[[Jedi Search]]'', the code that [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] uses to deactivate a [[EVS Construction Droid|construction droid]] is "SGW 0027." Each letter and number in the code is once removed from "THX 1138."<ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--February 1994-->
*In the novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] spies find schematics for [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] in "sector [[Tarp-Hard-Xenon|'''T'''-'''H'''ard-'''X'''enon]]" of a stolen [[Computer/Legends|computer]]. Also, a [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] guard named [[Thix|'''Th'''i'''x''']] is in charge of monitoring "sector '''1138'''."<ref name="SWArchive3">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/release/video/f20040909/index.html?page=3|text=Looking for THX 1138 in a Galaxy Far, Far Away…|date=2004-09-09}} {{C|Page 3 of 4}}</ref> <!--May 1996-->
*In ''[[Star Wars Missions 3: Attack on Delrakkin]]'', there is an Imperial [[pilot]] named [[2249]]--1138 with one added to each digit.<ref>''[[Star Wars Missions 3: Attack on Delrakkin]]''</ref> <!--November 1997-->
*In ''[[Darth Maul: Shadow Hunter]]'', [[I-5YQ]] informs [[Lorn Pavan]] that his bank fraud plan would be in violation of "communication protocols '''THX one-one-three'''—" but is cut off before he can say the inevitable eight.<ref name="SWArchive3" />
*In ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'', when reaching [[Dontamo]], [[Fy-Tor-Ana|Solace]] informs [[Oryon]] that [[Ferus Olin]]'s [[Starship|ship]] is on '''X'''YZ coordinates '''1138''', 1999, 2300.<ref>''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]''</ref> <!--April 2006-->
*The novel [[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] introduces a storage room aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] with the designation 3181, which is 1138 rearranged.<ref>[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']]</ref> <!--October 16, 2007-->
*In ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'', chapter 14, as [[Darman Skirata|Darman]] is going in search of fellow [[clone trooper]] [[Fi Skirata|Fi]] in the med bay, the med droid says that Fi is in "B eight, tank one-one-three," or Bay '''8''', [[Bacta tank/Legends|tank]] '''1-1-3'''.<ref>''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref> <!--October 30, 2007-->
*In ''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 2: Curse of the Black Hole Pirates]]'', it is stated that [[Cleaver]] has been trying to meditate for 46 [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], '''11''' [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] and '''38''' [[Standard second|seconds]].<ref name="Curse">''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 2: Curse of the Black Hole Pirates]]''</ref> <!--July 2010-->
*In the novel [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''The Force Unleashed II'']], chapter 8, [[Rahm Kota]]'s authorization code to land on the ''Salvation'' is "'''T'''alus '''H'''aroon '''Ten Eleven Thirty-eight'''."<ref>[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''The Force Unleashed II'']]</ref> As '''X''' is the Roman numeral for '''10''', you basically have '''T'''alus '''H'''aroon '''X-11-38'''. <!--October 2010-->

===Comics===
====Legends comics====
*In ''[[Gambler's World]]'', a newspaper comic strip later collected in ''[[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures]]'',<ref>[[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures 1|''Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures'' 1]]</ref> [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] are scrambling to respond to a priority "'''THX''' alert".<ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--March – September 1979-->
*Luke Skywalker finds himself trapped in section "'''TH-11'''" while infiltrating the [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] superweapon in [[Star Wars (1977) 52|''Star Wars 52: To Take The Tarkin'']].<ref>[[Star Wars (1977) 52|''Star Wars'' (1977) 52]]</ref>
*In ''[[Shadow Stalker]]'', there is an Imperial stormtrooper known as '''[[1138 (stormtrooper)|1138]]'''.<ref name="Stalker">''[[Shadow Stalker]]''</ref> <!--January 1997 / November 1997-->
*In the comic story "[[Three Against the Galaxy]]," published in [[Star Wars Tales 3|''Star Wars Tales'' 3]], there is a tunnel called '''[[THX 8]]'''.<ref>{{Tales|3|Three Against the Galaxy}}</ref>
*In the comic issue [[Republic 47|''Republic'' 47]], there is a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] model called a [[THX servant droid|'''THX''' servant droid]].<ref name="Republic 47">[[Republic 47|''Republic'' 47]]</ref>
*In [[Republic 68|''Republic'' 68]],<ref name="Rep68">[[Republic 68|''Republic'' 68]]</ref> there is a bioweapon identified as [[Trihexalophine1138|'''t'''ri'''h'''e'''x'''alophine'''1138''']].<ref name="SW" /> <!--August 2004-->
*In non-canon [[Infinities]] comic story ''[[Fett Club (comic story)|Fett Club]]'', featured in [[Star Wars Tales 24|''Star Wars Tales'' 24]], [[Fett Club]]'s address is "'''1138'''."<ref>{{Tales|24|Fett Club (comic story)}}</ref> <!--July 2005-->
*In the comic [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'']], chapter 8, [[Rahm Kota]]'s authorization code to land on the ''Salvation'' is "'''T'''alus '''H'''aroon 10-'''11-38'''."<ref>[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'']]</ref> As '''X''' is the Roman numeral for '''10''', you basically have '''T'''alus '''H'''aroon '''X-11-38'''. <!--September 2010-->
*In the comic short story "[[Bane vs ...Bane?]]," published in [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.46|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic UK 6.46'']], there is a docking bay called [[Docking bay TH X-11-38|Docking bay '''TH X-11-38''']].<ref>{{TCWUKCite|6.46|Bane vs ...Bane?}}</ref> <!--May 2013-->

====Canon comics====
*In [[Jedi Knights 5|''Jedi Knights'' 5]], [[Nute Gunray]] mentions a trade deal involving '''1''','''138''' dynesium parcels.<ref name="JK5">[[Jedi Knights 5|''Jedi Knights'' 5]]</ref>

===Short stories===
*In the Legends short story "[[Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd]]," originally published online by [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]],<ref name="SPL">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20090531/index.html?page=1|text=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd: A Hyperspace Exclusive|int=Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd}}</ref> there is a book called ''[[One Thousand, One Hundred and Thirty-Eight Wonders of the Ancient Galaxy|'''One Thousand, One Hundred and Thirty-Eight''' Wonders of the Ancient Galaxy]]''.<ref name="SW" />

===Guide books===
*In the Legends guide book ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: The Visual Guide]]'', there is a battle droid known as '''[[1138 (Confederacy of Independent Systems)|1138]]'''.<ref>''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: The Visual Guide]]''</ref>

===Roleplaying games===
====West End Games====
*In ''[[The Isis Coordinates]]'', one successful outcome of the story is the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Desolator (Imperial-class)|''Desolator'']] colliding with a [[Star/Legends|star]] named [[G-138|G-'''138''']].<ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--June 1990-->
*In [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a wanted [[Painting/Legends|poster]] for [[Qan Gindoch]] shows the number 9573'''THX1138'''C25N printed on his mugshot.<ref name="SWArchive3" /> <!--June 1993-->
*In ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', a character sheet for an "[[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|ISB]] Investigations Specialist" contains a quote by "[[Specialist 1138|Specialist '''1138''']]."<ref name="Heroes">''[[Heroes & Rogues]]''</ref> <!--December 1995-->
*In "[[Special Ops: The Art of Infiltration]]," an article published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 17|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 17]], a cancelled issue of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', [[Patrol 1138|Patrol '''1138''']] is a five-man patrol of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref>{{Journal|17|Special Ops: The Art of Infiltration}}</ref>

====Wizards of the Coast====
*In the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' on all pages you can find number the 1138.<ref name="SWArchive3" />
*The ''[[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|Living Force]]'' roleplaying module ''[[Broken Orbits: Tilnes Rising]]'' references the [[Thaereian military]] directive [[Order 1138]]<ref name="Tilnes Rising">''[[Broken Orbits: Tilnes Rising]]''</ref> while the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Looking In: Outward Bound]]'' features [[docking bay 1138]], a the [[Hangar/Legends|landing pad]] on the planet [[Druckenwell/Legends|Druckenwell]].<ref name="Outward Bound">''[[Looking In: Outward Bound]]''</ref>
*In the [[Wizards.com]] online article [[Destabilization (article)|''Destabilization'']], there is a [[HoloNet]] node called "tee-aitch-dot-ex-one-dot-one-dot-three-dot-eight", or '''th.x.1.1.3.8'''.<ref>{{WizardsCite|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20040105|text=Destabilization|int=Destabilization (article)}}</ref>
*The Wizards.com article
*[[Block 1138 Sable|Block '''1138''' Sable]] was a room block on the [[Errant Venture/Legends|''Errant Venture'']] in the roleplaying sourcebook [[Scum and Villainy (sourcebook)|''Scum and Villainy'']].<ref>[[Scum and Villainy (sourcebook)|''Scum and Villainy'']]</ref>

===Card games===
*Though not stated in the film itself, the stilt-legged [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] that is parked outside the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina|Mos Eisley cantina]] in {{Film|4}}, is referred to as a "[[Void Spider TX-3 Air Taxi|''Void Spider'' '''''THX 1138''''']]" in [[Decipher, Inc.|Decipher's]] [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="SW" /><ref name="ANH Limited">{{CCG|set=A New Hope Limited|cardname=Bespin Motors Void Spider THX 1138}}</ref>
*Although it is not stated in the film, the [[Young Jedi Collectible Card Game]] states that one of the [[Vulture-class starfighter|Vulture Droids]] that attacks the [[Radiant VII/Legends|Republic space cruiser]] at the beginning of ''The Phantom Menace'' is named [[DFS-1138|DFS-'''1138''']].<ref name="SW" /><ref name="Young">''[[Young Jedi Collectible Card Game]]'' {{C|Card: [http://www.decipher.com/youngjedi/cardlists/duelofthefates/dark/large/starfighterdroiddfs1138.html DFS-1138]}}</ref>

===Theme park attractions===
*In the simulator theme park [[Star Tours (real-world)|Star Tours]], a public address announcer can be heard warning the owner of a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] named '''[[THX 1138 (landspeeder)|THX 1138]]''', saying that the vehicle is parked in a [[no-hover area]].<ref name="SWArchive3" />
*[[Spaceport THX1138|Spaceport '''THX1138''']] is a terminal for [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] and other travel agencies in the simulator ride theme park [[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]].<ref>{{SW|url=news/convenient-daily-departures-the-history-of-star-tours|date=2013-08-22|text=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of ''Star Tours''|int=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours|nolive=1}}</ref> [[Star Tours Flight 1138|Star Tours Flight '''1138''']] is a flight itinerary to [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]].<ref>[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]</ref>

===StarWars.com===
*An e-card released on [[StarWars.com]] for Father's Day says [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] has '''1138''' messages.<ref name="Ecard">{{SWArchive|url=play/ecards/holidays_dad_wanted-to-say.html|text=Father's Day: Wanted to Say}}</ref>
